The Hundred-Foot Journey (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $54,235,441Details
International Box Office $40,035,047
Worldwide Box Office $94,270,488
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $19,110,459
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,261,790
Total $22,372,249
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Hassan Haji and his family are displaced from their native India to a small French town, where they plan to open an Indian restaurant. Across the street, Madame Mallory already runs one of the most respected French restaurants in the country, and when she catches wind of it, battle commences.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$10,979,290 (20.2% of total gross)
Legs:4.94 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:57.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$22,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,023 opening theaters/2,167 max. theaters, 7.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $79,394,845

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 8th, 2014 (Wide) by Walt Disney
International Releases: August 14th, 2014 (Wide) (New Zealand)
August 15th, 2014 (Wide), released as The Hundred-foot Journey (Australia)
August 21st, 2014 (Wide) (Germany)
October 9th, 2014 (Wide) (Netherlands)
May 8th, 2025 (Limited) (Australia)
Video Release: December 2nd, 2014 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for thematic elements, some violence, language and brief sensuality.
(Rating bulletin 2326, 6/11/2014)
Running Time: 122 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Intertitle, Prologue, Voiceover/Narration
Geographical setting: France
Plot point: Culture Clash, December Romance, Food, Immigration, Indians, Interracial Romance, Professional Rivalry, Romance, Romeo and Juliet Romance, Widow/Widower
Subgenre: Comedy Drama
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: DreamWorks Pictures, Reliance Entertainment, Participant Media, Image Nation, Amblin Entertainment, Harpo Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English, Hindi, French
